t_ch: 好漫长一段记忆，恍若隔世…
t_en: That was such a long memory, it seemed like a completely different world...

t_ch: 漫长？你在说什么？
t_en: Long? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 你刚才就呆站在这里，无论我们怎么呼喊都不回应。
t_en: You were standing here motionless just now, no matter how much we shouted, you didn't respond.

t_ch: 状况持续了不到一分钟，然后……
t_en: It hadn't even been a full minute when...

t_ch: …然后这个生物就出现了。
t_en: ...This creature appeared.

t_ch: 真是奇特的动物——应该是动物吧？我从没见过。
t_en: Such a unique animal. I think it's an animal? I have never seen anything like it before.

t_ch: 可爱的小家伙…它是{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的馈赠吗？
t_en: What a cute little creature... Is it a gift from {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 我被浮黎瞥视了。
t_en: Fuli gazed at me.

t_ch: 浮黎？刚才？
t_en: Fuli? Just now?

t_ch: 「浮黎」…那是什么？
t_en: Fuli... What's that?

t_ch: 前往奥赫玛的途中，我曾和你聊起天外的「星神」——浮黎就是其中之一，掌管「记忆」命途。
t_en: Remember the Aeons that I spoke to you about on the way to Okhema? Fuli is one of them and presides over the Path of Remembrance.

t_ch: 啊…「天外之界的神明，难以用肉眼直视的奇迹」…对吗？
t_en: Ahh... A god from the worlds beyond the sky. A miracle that is hard to see with the naked eye... Right?

t_ch: 能获得星神的瞥视，是否就意味着……
t_en: To earn the gaze of an Aeon, does that mean...

t_ch: 迷迷说它能帮助我们。
t_en: Mem says that they can help us.

t_ch: 该去对付尼卡多利了。
t_en: It's time for us to deal with Nikador.

t_ch: 泰坦，过往…迷雾。
t_en: Titan, past... Foggy.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>收集。过去…复现。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Collect. Past... Reappear.</color>

t_ch: 我们需要收集悬锋城的记忆。
t_en: We must collect the memories of Kremnos.

t_ch: 我们需要让过去重现。
t_en: We have to recreate the past.

t_ch: 你能听懂它的话？
t_en: You can understand what they are saying?

t_ch: 收集记忆，重现过去…该怎么做？我们不剩多少时间了。
t_en: Collect the memories and recreate the past... How can we do that? We don't have much time left.

t_ch: 着急，不必！我会。教你。
t_en: Anxious, no need! I will. Teach you.

t_ch: 迷迷会教我们该怎么做。
t_en: Mem will tell us what we have to do.

t_ch: 跟上它吧，没有更好的办法。
t_en: Let's follow them. We don't have any better ideas right now.

t_ch: 如果它真是浮黎的造物，亦或和那位星神存在某种关联，我愿意相信它能施展比「{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}奇迹」更强大的力量。
t_en: If they are truly a creation of Fuli, or have some connection to that Aeon, I'm willing to believe they can wield a power greater than {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s Miracle.

t_ch: 走吧，我们跟上它。
t_en: Let's go, we need to follow them.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}…你睡下了吗？
t_en: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}... Have you fallen into a slumber?

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 我不知道你口中的「母亲」是谁，但……
t_en: I don't know who the "Mother" that you're referring to is, but...

t_ch: 一直以来辛苦你了。如果你也会做梦…希望你的梦是甜美的。
t_en: Thank you for all your hard work. If Titans can dream... May your dream be a sweet one.

t_ch: 这是我们来时的那条路。
t_en: This is the path we took when we first arrived.

t_ch: 没错…世界仿佛在一晃之间便走入了末路。
t_en: It is... The world seems to have plummeted to its end in the blink of an eye.

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 「我诅骂你们，凡人…诅骂你们的傲慢，你们的残酷。」
t_en: "Curse you mortals... curse your pride and your merciless ways."

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: …抱歉，我不想转译那些字眼。
t_en: ...Please pardon me. Words this vile should not be relayed.

t_ch: 看来想获得{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的帮助绝非易事。
t_en: It's not going to be easy getting {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s help.

t_ch: 总算出来了…那遗迹里真是一团糟。
t_en: At last, we're out... That place was in total disarray.

t_ch: 错乱的时空…大概也映射了{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的扭曲和挣扎。
t_en: The distorted time and space... is likely a reflection of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s own twisted struggles.

t_ch: 一片静谧。
t_en: The silence is deafening.

t_ch: 这里…曾经不是这样。
t_en: This place... isn't as I knew it.

t_ch: 这里也有被遗弃的扎格列斯之手，借用一下吧。
t_en: There's an abandoned Hand of Zagreus here. Let's use it.

t_ch: 前面有路障。
t_en: There's an obstacle ahead.

t_ch: 用「奇迹宝珠」或许能映照出通路。
t_en: Perhaps the Miracle Orbs can illuminate the way forward.

t_ch: 看，预言算碑。说不定可以靠它来打开栅栏。
t_en: There, a Prophecy Tablet. It could be the key to unlocking the way.

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 「你们以为自己赢了？傲慢…愚蠢……」
t_en: "You think that you've won? Arrogant... Foolish..."

t_ch: 还在硬撑吗，泰坦？
t_en: Titan, are you still trying to hold out?

t_ch: 居然是这样的解法…幸好有丹恒和{NICKNAME}在我们身边。
t_en: That was an unexpected solution... Thankfully, we had Dan Heng and {NICKNAME} here with us.

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 我们通过了它的考验，我想{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}有些惊诧。
t_en: I think that even {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} themself is surprised that we managed to clear the challenge.

t_ch: 至少它遵守了承诺，打开了谒见的道路。
t_en: At least they kept their promise and opened the path for an audience.

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: This...

t_ch: 像是一股寒流渗入了脑海，一双长着百指的手在搅动回忆。
t_en: It feels like a cold wave is seeping into the mind, and a hand with countless fingers is churning through my memories.

t_ch: 那些拥有美满人生的人，或许会享受这种干预吧……
t_en: Maybe people who have led wonderful lives would enjoy such an intrusion...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 「离开…离开。」
t_en: "Leave... leave."

t_ch: 帮助我们吧，泰坦！为了防止这个世界因疯狂毁于一旦！
t_en: Help us, Titan! Help us prevent this world from being ruined by madness!

t_ch: （泰坦的低语）
t_en: *Titan's murmurs*

t_ch: 「拒绝…拒绝。」
t_en: "Refuse... refuse."

t_ch: （第一次感觉，列车如此陌生……）
t_en: (It's the first time that the Express has felt so unfamiliar...)

t_ch: （想去观景车厢看看……）
t_en: (I want to take a look in the Parlor Car...)

t_ch: 啊，怎么又让我做场外支援？真无聊……
t_en: Ugh, why do I have to be the standby support again? So boring...

t_ch: 战斗…同伴，一起！
t_en: Battle... Companions, together!

t_ch: 不留活口…对吗？
t_en: Leave no survivors... Right?

t_ch: 杀戮的音乐，多动听啊。
t_en: The melody of slaughter, how beautiful.

t_ch: 该收尸了……
t_en: It's time to clean up...

t_ch: 共赏？你们不配！
t_en: Savor it? You're unworthy!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们一同起舞吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's dance together.

t_ch: 和我一起——点燃大海。
t_en: Together, we shall... set the seas ablaze.

t_ch: 我们是…同伴。
t_en: We are... companions.

t_ch: 这篇剧本…我就是主角！
t_en: In this script... I am the main character!

t_ch: 看招，系统崩溃！
t_en: Take this. System crash!

t_ch: 立正挨打！
t_en: Stand still so I can beat you!

t_ch: 玩的就是个刺激！
t_en: It's all about that adrenaline rush!

t_ch: 骇入成功，状态回满！
t_en: Hack successful. Condition recovered!

t_ch: 怪我作弊？哼，有种来封我的号啊！
t_en: Blaming me for cheating? Hmph, ban my account if you dare!

t_ch: 还好不用再骑大地兽进城了。辛苦了，缇宝老师。
t_en: Thank goodness we no longer have to ride the dromas into the city. Thanks for your hard work, Tribbie.

t_ch: 这种时候就别说客气话了。你们找到解决方法了，对吧？
t_en: No need for formalities at a time like this. I trust that you have found a solution?

t_ch: 长话短说：{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}不愿帮我们揭露悬锋城的过去。但不知为何，它对{NICKNAME}展现出了兴趣。
t_en: Simply put, {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} refused to help us uncover the past of Castrum Kremnos. But for some reason, they showed interest toward {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 它以…某种方式…赠予了{NICKNAME}这只宠物——
t_en: They... through some unknown means... gifted {NICKNAME} this pet—

t_ch: 宠物。迷迷——不是！
t_en: Pet. Mem — isn't!

t_ch: ——按丹恒的说法，这只生物和天外的神明有关。兴许能解决我们的麻烦。
t_en: According to Dan Heng, this creature is related to a god from beyond the sky. Perhaps they can help us solve the problem.

t_ch: 天外的神明……
t_en: A god from beyond the sky...

t_ch: 缇宝老师，接下来我想兵分两路：由我立刻动身，赶往悬锋城支援万敌。
t_en: Tribbie, let's split up here. I'll make my way to Kremnos immediately to lend my support to Mydei.

t_ch: 而寻找{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}弱点的任务…{NICKNAME}，可以拜托你吗？这是我发自肺腑的请求。
t_en: As for the mission of finding out {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s weakness... Can I leave it to you, {NICKNAME}? I ask this sincerely from the bottom of my heart.

t_ch: 包在我和迷迷身上吧。
t_en: Just leave it to me and Mem.

t_ch: 当心，你可不是不死之身。
t_en: Be careful, you're not indestructible.

t_ch: 放心吧，我最擅长的事就是「大难不死」。
t_en: Don't worry about me, surviving the impossible is my specialty.

t_ch: 我和万敌在战场上等你。
t_en: Mydei and I will be waiting for you on the battlefield.

t_ch: 也带上我吧，白厄。与其在后方等待，不如让我也上前线，为你们分担压力。
t_en: Let me come too, Phainon. I'd rather be on the front lines, helping ease the burden.

t_ch: …谢谢，我没有拒绝的理由。
t_en: ...Thank you. I don't have any reason to refuse.

t_ch: 那就在此别过吧——我和丹恒即刻前往悬锋城，{NICKNAME}想办法破解蛮神不灭之躯的秘密。遐蝶小姐……
t_en: Then this is where we part ways — Dan Heng and I will head to Castrum Kremnos immediately, while {NICKNAME} works on uncovering the secret behind the barbaric god's indestructible body. As for miss Castorice...

t_ch: 我会跟在{NICKNAME}身边，保护{M#他}{F#她}的安全。
t_en: I'll stay with {NICKNAME} to ensure {M#his}{F#her} safety.

t_ch: 拜托了。缇宝老师——能再劳烦你一次吗？
t_en: I'll leave it to you. Tribbie... Could I trouble you once again?

t_ch: 当然，「百界门」就交给*我们*吧。
t_en: Of course, just leave the Century Gate to us.

t_ch: 现在只剩我们了…该去哪寻找消失的记忆呢？
t_en: We're the only ones left... Where should we go to find the lost memories?

t_ch: 记忆…气味，浓烈。
t_en: Memories... Smell, intense.

t_ch: 追踪，找到。往日…重现。
t_en: Tracking, found. Past... Recreate.

t_ch: 相信迷迷的嗅觉吧。
t_en: Let's just believe in Mem's sense of smell.

t_ch: 迷迷，相信。跟上，伙伴！
t_en: Mem, believe. Follow, friends!

t_ch: 所以你其实是只小狗？
t_en: So, you're actually a dog?

t_ch: 宠物，不是！
t_en: Pet, not!

t_ch: 迷迷，相信。跟上，伙伴！
t_en: Mem, believe. Follow, friends!

t_ch: 与此同时……
t_en: In the meantime...

t_ch: 障碍物挡住了去路…或许可以使用二楼的推球将箱子击碎。
t_en: Obstacles are blocking the path... Maybe the ball on the second floor can be utilized to break the box.

t_ch: 喔，这位温婉优雅的女士！您也是来听利波献唱的吗？
t_en: Oh, elegant lady! Are you here to listen to Lipos' song?

t_ch: 您想听些什么？古老黄金裔的情与爱…黄金战争的乱世…沿海百邦的崛起与没落…利波这里应有尽有！
t_en: What are you in the mood for? The love stories of the ancient Chrysos Heirs? The turbulent era of the Chrysos War? The rise and fall of the Seaside States? Lipos has it all!

t_ch: 请问…有没有诗歌描绘了{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的疯狂，还有悬锋城隐于迷雾的秘密？
t_en: I wonder if there is a ballad about {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s madness as well as the mystery of Castrum Kremnos vanishing into the fog.

t_ch: 女士，您真是找对了人！利波的收藏中有这样一首诗歌，还从未飘入过他人的耳廓。
t_en: Ma'am, you have come to the right place! There is such a ballad within my collection that has yet to grace the ears of others.

t_ch: 那么，就由我来为各位吟诵——
t_en: Allow me to sing it for you—

t_ch: 「千年的战争进入尾声，世界即将遁入混沌长夜；骁勇英武的泰坦啊，你金黄的心灵因何变得支离破碎？」
t_en: "The war of a thousand years has drawn to its close. And now the world descends into a long night of chaos. O valiant Titan, tell me, how did your golden heart shatter into a thousand fragments?"

t_ch: 「神明啊，你厌倦了纷争吗？若是如此，该如何解释你的暴戾，笼罩你的幽暗该如何祛散？」
t_en: "O god, have you grown weary of endless strife? Then how do you justify your ruthless tyranny? And how shall you cast off the darkness that so deeply shrouds your divinity?"

t_ch: 「神明啊，你遗忘了荣耀吗？若是如此，该如何守护你的尊严，敬畏你的子民该去向何方？」
t_en: "O god, have you forgotten the glory of old? Then how will you defend your dignity? And where shall your faithful subjects find their home?"

t_ch: 「噢，伟大的{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}…我明白，我们都明白啊。你那不屈的神性并未死去，你只是暂时被那汹涌的黑潮给缠住了啊。」
t_en: "O mighty {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, the truth is clear to us all. Your steadfast divinity is not vanquished. You are shrouded momentarily by that encroaching black tide."

t_ch: 「但你从未屈服！你也从未临阵脱逃过啊。你金黄的战士之魂，也终有一日会战胜黑潮，重塑那悬锋的王座啊。」
t_en: "But you never faltered, nor did you flee! Your golden warrior spirit, brave and bold, will conquer the black tide, as foretold. To the throne of Kremnos, you'll return once more!"

t_ch: 「我们的神，我们的王，为了支撑你永恒的战斗，我们准备了盛大的祭礼啊——」
t_en: "Our god, our king, to aid your timeless struggle, we have prepared a grand ritual—"

t_ch: 「请允许我们用卑微的十指触碰你的灵魂，为你编织盛装神性的剑皿…它将赐予你永恒的生命，贮存你永恒的荣耀啊。」
t_en: "Please grant us the honor to touch your soul with our humble hands, and present to you this sacred Sword Vessel... It shall consecrate your eternal life and enshrine your everlasting glory."

t_ch: 这样，完成。记忆，收集。
t_en: There, done. Memory, collected.

t_ch: 味道，浓烈…继续，下一个！
t_en: Scent, strong... Onward, next!

t_ch: 感谢你，拥有美妙嗓音的诗人。你的诗歌会被永远传唱下去，成为保卫奥赫玛的壁垒。
t_en: Thank you, esteemed bard with an enchanting voice. Your ballads will endure through the ages, serving as a bulwark for Okhema.

t_ch: 哈托努斯…这么说来，我是听说过它为悬锋城打造圣器的故事。
t_en: Chartonus... Come to think of it, I remember hearing about them making a holy vessel for Kremnos.

t_ch: 噢…遐蝶。异乡人，还有。
t_en: Oh... Castorice. Outlander, as well.

t_ch: 找哈托努斯，有何贵干？
t_en: To Chartonus, what brings you?

t_ch: 我记得，您曾受悬锋族人之邀，为他们打造某样圣物…不知您能否回忆起其中细节？
t_en: I remember the Kremnoans commissioned you to craft a divine object before. Do you happen to recall the details?

t_ch: 啊…此事，确有。你们为何问及？
t_en: Ah... Happened it did. Why ask, do you?

t_ch: 为了彻底打败尼卡多利。
t_en: To vanquish Nikador for good.

t_ch: 为了保护奥赫玛。
t_en: To protect Okhema.
Hm...

t_ch: 千百件之多，我曾打造过的神兵利器。逐一记住，不可能。
t_en: Thousands of marvels of weaponry, I crafted. Remember them all, impossible it is.

t_ch: 异乡人，致歉。帮助，我难以给予。
t_en: Outlander, forgive me. Help, I cannot.

t_ch: 的确…大工匠活了太久，不可能记住经手过的每一件委托。还有别的方法吗……
t_en: That makes sense. With such a long life, the Grand Craftsman can't remember every single item he's made. Is there another way?

t_ch: 迷迷，有办法！
t_en: Mem, knows!

t_ch: 帮助，伙伴！回忆…找回来！
t_en: Help, friend! Memory, retrieve!

t_ch: 如何，大工匠？用于封印神性的「剑皿」，你造得出来吗？
t_en: Grand Craftsman, would it be possible for you to craft a Sword Vessel that can seal divinity?

t_ch: 我的锻锤无法打造的物件，不存在。
t_en: Anything my forging hammer cannot craft, there is not.

t_ch: 悬锋城最上等的石料，拿来。纷争泰坦的黄金血，找来。剑皿，我会锻造。
t_en: The finest stones in Kremnos, give me. The golden blood of Strife Titan, find me. The Sword Vessel, I shall craft.

t_ch: 看这意思，你答应下来了？
t_en: Does that mean we have a deal?

t_ch: 那报酬呢？我们在请求你打造一件惊世的圣器…不可能不支付酬劳。
t_en: And what of your payment? For a commission of this magnitude, proper compensation must be ensured.

t_ch: 酬劳…简单。
t_en: Payment... simple.

t_ch: 和平，给我的族人。山之民，不再做悬锋城的奴隶或斗士，永远。
t_en: Peace, for my people. Mountain Dwellers, no longer servants or fighters for Kremnos. Ever.

t_ch: …我答应你，大工匠。
t_en: ...You have my word, Grand Craftsman.

t_ch: 这样，完成。记忆，收集。
t_en: There, done. Memory, collected.

t_ch: 味道，浓烈…继续，下一个！
t_en: Scent, strong... Onward, next!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，你还好吗？刚才……
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, are you feeling alright? You were—

t_ch: 我看到了大工匠的记忆。
t_en: I saw the memory of the Grand Craftsman.

t_ch: 啊…是这个小家伙的能力吗？真令人吃惊。
t_en: Ah, was it because of this little one? That's incredible!

t_ch: 窥探他人记忆的力量，听起来很容易被滥用。幸好掌握它的人是你。
t_en: The ability to look into others' memories sounds like something that could easily be misused. Luckily, you're the one wielding it.

t_ch: 记忆，观察。幸福…带去！
t_en: Memory, observed. Happiness... take!

t_ch: 这里。这里！
t_en: Here. Here!

t_ch: 记忆，照片…收集！
t_en: Memories, photos... Collect!

t_ch: 那是…达米亚诺斯？阿格莱雅女士已经将他释放了吗？
t_en: Is that... Damionis? Did Lady Aglaea release him already?

t_ch: 那家伙，差点没把我们害死！
t_en: That guy... He almost got us killed!

t_ch: 他的确背负了些不好的名声…若不是他的作为，你和丹恒阁下也不会遭受阿格莱雅大人的审讯。
t_en: He certainly has a bad reputation. If it weren't for him, you and Dan Heng wouldn't have been interrogated by Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 这哥们，一点版权意识没有。
t_en: Has that dude never heard of copyright before?

t_ch: 嗯，我听过和他有关的负面传闻…他有美学上的才能，但若不是他的作为，你和丹恒阁下也不会遭受阿格莱雅大人的审讯。
t_en: I've heard some bad things about him. He may have some talent in the arts, but if it weren't for him, you and Dan Heng wouldn't have been interrogated by Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 如果迷迷认定他藏有宝贵的记忆，那就当面询问一番吧。
t_en: But if Mem thinks he has some valuable memories, we'd better ask him in person.

t_ch: …嗯？是谁？我正忙着取景，请别……
t_en: ...Hmm? Who's that? I'm busy working on a scene, please don't...

t_ch: 啊，居然是「异乡人」阁下，别来无恙啊？离我们上次见面还没过去多久……
t_en: Ah, it's the esteemed "outlander." How've you been? It hasn't been all that long since we last met...

t_ch: 之前差点被你害死！
t_en: You almost got me killed!

t_ch: 非常抱歉，之前都是我的错，我一心只想证明「天外之界」的存在……
t_en: My apologies, that was entirely my fault. I was determined to prove that a world beyond the sky exists...

t_ch: 幸好阿格莱雅大人十分仁慈。她只是让我立下誓言，然后就放我回家了。
t_en: Fortunately, Lady Aglaea is quite lenient. She let me go after I made a vow.

t_ch: 你这么快就自由了？
t_en: You're out already?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人十分仁慈。她只是让我立下誓言，然后就放我回家了。
t_en: Lady Aglaea is quite lenient. She let me go after I made a vow.

t_ch: 请放心吧！同样的错误，我绝不再犯第二次。退一万步说，假如我敢打破和那位女士的约定，那和自掘坟墓有什么区别？
t_en: Rest assured, I'm not foolish enough to make the same mistake twice. Breaking a vow with Lady Aglaea would be like... digging my own grave.

t_ch: 你有和悬锋城相关的记忆吗？
t_en: Do you have any memories related to Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 悬锋城？对于我们这些手无寸铁的平民，{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的领土可是禁地。就算是在从前……
t_en: Castrum Kremnos? For us unarmed civilians, {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s territory is off-limits. Even in the past...

t_ch: …等等，你提醒了我，阁下…我的确去过悬锋城。请给我片刻……
t_en: Oh, n–no... Wait, I just remembered! I did visit Castrum Kremnos once. Let me find it for you...

t_ch: 没错…对，就是这张！不可思议，我居然还留着它……
t_en: Y–Yes, yes, this is the one! I can't believe I still have it...

t_ch: 请问，这幅作品有什么故事？
t_en: Could you tell us the story behind it?

t_ch: 很多年前，黄金战争接近尾声时，悬锋城举办了最后一次祭典。但以当时的局势，根本没多少异邦的公民敢去参加竞赛。
t_en: During the final days of the Chrysos War, the final festival took place in Castrum Kremnos. However, due to the tense situation, few outsiders were willing to compete.

t_ch: 我鼓起勇气前往悬锋城，却发现那祭典的氛围…不同往常。悬锋族人更像是在准备什么神秘的仪式。
t_en: I gathered the courage to go there, but I found the atmosphere of the festival to be... quite strange. It felt more like the Kremnoans were preparing for a mysterious ceremony.

t_ch: 我感到事态蹊跷，只在城外拍了张照片就离开了…谁能想到后面发生的事呢？
t_en: Sensing something was off, I snapped a photo outside the city and then left... And what followed was something completely unexpected.

t_ch: 能将这张照片交给我们吗？我们…正在收集那个年代的线索。
t_en: Would you mind giving us the photo? We're... trying to piece together clues from that time.

t_ch: 当然，请拿去吧。如果可以，请把它当作我的补偿。
t_en: Sure, take it! Think of it as my way of making it up to you.

t_ch: 完成。记忆，收集。
t_en: Done. Memory, collected.

t_ch: 味道，浓烈…继续，下一个！
t_en: Scent, strong... Onward, next!

t_ch: 记忆…收集，完成！
t_en: Memory... collected!

t_ch: 悬锋…前往！帮助，伙伴！
t_en: Kremnos... go! Help, friend!

t_ch: 它又说了什么，{NICKNAME}阁下？
t_en: What did they say, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 记忆已经收集完成…
t_en: All memories have been collected...

t_ch: …该出发去悬锋城了。
t_en: ...It's time to head to Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 准备工作已经完成了吗？那该去跟白厄和万敌阁下汇合了。
t_en: Is everything in order? If so, we need to meet up with Lords Phainon and Mydei.

t_ch: 动身前…我想再和阿格莱雅大人确认一番。请稍等，{NICKNAME}阁下。
t_en: Before we leave, I'd like to check with Lady Aglaea again. Do you mind waiting for me, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 这样就可以了。我们出发吧，{NICKNAME}阁下。
t_en: Everything's set now. Let's head out, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你害怕阿格莱雅吗？
t_en: Are you scared of Lady Aglaea?

t_ch: 害怕？不…我只是不想看见她失落的样貌。
t_en: Scared? No, I just don't want to see her disappointed.

t_ch: 你每件事都要向她汇报吗？
t_en: Do you always have to report everything to her?

t_ch: 我只是不想看见她失落的样貌。
t_en: I just don't want to see her disappointed.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人，她为奥赫玛做出的奉献值得更多感激。但大多数人只记住了她冰冷的一面。
t_en: Lady Aglaea's contributions to Okhema deserve more appreciation, but people mostly see her as distant and aloof.

t_ch: 现在不是说这些的时候…该出发了。希望这个小家伙能帮我们复现悬锋城的记忆。
t_en: We don't have time to talk about this right now... Let's set off. Hopefully, this little one will help us recreate the memories of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 伙伴，相信！出发，悬锋！
t_en: Friend, trust! Go, Kremnos!

t_ch: 战斗仍在继续…听上去相当激烈。
t_en: The battle is still raging... I can feel the intensity even from here.

t_ch: 真想去正面支援他们啊…
t_en: I wish I could go and help them...

t_ch: 我能理解，{NICKNAME}阁下…但眼下我们还要奔赴另一片战场。
t_en: I understand how you feel, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}. But right now, we must head to another battlefield.

t_ch: 我们也有自己的战场。
t_en: We have our own battle to face.

t_ch: 没错，只有完成被托付的任务，才能真正帮到他们。
t_en: Exactly. We'll only be able to help them once we've seen our own mission through.

t_ch: 至少说明他们都还活着。
t_en: At least we know they're still alive.

t_ch: 想帮助他们，我们必须完成被托付的任务。
t_en: We'll only be able to help them once we've seen our own mission through.

t_ch: 这里，记忆，完整。交给我，碎片。
t_en: Here, memory, complete. Give me, fragments.

t_ch: 往世…由我，复现。
t_en: Past... I, recreate.

t_ch: 记忆，完整。交给我，碎片。
t_en: Memory, complete. Give me, fragments.

t_ch: 往世…复现，由我。
t_en: Past... Recreate, I will.

t_ch: 碎片，完整。记忆…涌现。
t_en: Fragments, complete. Memory... appear.

t_ch: 往世，入口…打开！
t_en: Past, entrance... Open!

t_ch: 那就是通向往世的门吗？
t_en: Is that the gate to the past?

t_ch: 走吧，{NICKNAME}阁下。我们一起发掘{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的秘密。
t_en: Let's go, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}. We will unravel {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s secrets together.

t_ch: 记住，阿格莱雅，无论黄金裔还是元老院，我们的地位都是由奥赫玛的公民赋予的。
t_en: Remember, Aglaea, whether it be the Chrysos Heirs or the Council, our positions are entrusted to us by the people of Okhema.

t_ch: 在你妄图僭越，做出超越本分的判断前，<color=#dbc291ff>仔细回想下黄金战争吧，想想那些「英雄」最后的下场。</color>
t_en: Before you attempt to overreach and make decisions beyond your place, <color=#dbc291ff>take a moment to recall the Chrysos War and what became of those "heroes."</color>

t_ch: 要是你对那段历史不甚熟悉，就让她再给你上上课吧。
t_en: If you're not well-versed in that history, have her refresh your memory.

t_ch: 请放心，凯妮斯阁下，在神谕的指引下，我们会推翻诸神；而在预言承诺的新世界，将不再有神明凌驾于凡人之上。
t_en: Do not be concerned, Elder Caenis. Following the prophecy's guidance, we shall cast down the gods — in the new world, no god will stand above humankind.

t_ch: 呵…我就姑且收下你的承诺吧。
t_en: Heh... I'll hold you to that promise for now.

t_ch: 当年娇生惯养的女孩，如今也长成了精明的政治动物。该说是可贺？还是可悲？
t_en: The pampered little girl has grown up to be a shrewd politician. Should I be pleased or concerned?

t_ch: 时代在剧变，人亦需要适应，我不是唯一一个下定决心改变的人，元老阁下。
t_en: Times are changing drastically, and people must adapt. I'm not the only one committed to making a difference, Elder Caenis.

t_ch: 我和吾师还有必须处理的要事，恕不远送。
t_en: My teacher and I have other pressing matters to attend to. Please see yourself out.

t_ch: 呵…我衷心希望，下次召开元老议会时，在圆环中央接受审判的不会是你。
t_en: Heh... I genuinely hope that at the next Council meeting, you won't be the one standing in the middle, facing the judgment.

t_ch: 阿雅…没事吧？
t_en: Agy... Are you alright?

t_ch: 不过又是空洞的威慑，和以往并无不同…都是他们内心恐惧的显化。
t_en: It's nothing more than an empty threat, much like before — a reflection of their inner fears.

t_ch: 真对不起，阿雅…如果*我们*能再成熟些，就不用让你一个人承担所有了……
t_en: So sorry, Agy... If we were more mature, you wouldn't have to face all this alone.

t_ch: 为何要道歉？你我只是在各司其职。
t_en: Why apologize? We each have our own responsibilities.

t_ch: 但是，看见你一直被他们刁难…*我们*还是会难过，也会气愤……
t_en: But seeing how they give you a hard time... makes us sad, and angry...

t_ch: 你希望我用金线割开他们的喉咙吗？这易如反掌。只需要你的认同，我便能让元老院成为历史。
t_en: Would you like me to slice their throats with golden threads? It would be done in a flash. All I need is your blessing, and the Council will be no more.

t_ch: 你、你在说什么可怕的话啊！别、别这样吓*我们*，阿雅！
t_en: Wh—What are you talking about!? D—Don't scare us like that, Agy!

t_ch: 哈哈，开玩笑的。
t_en: Haha, I'm just teasing you.

t_ch: 就知道你在开玩笑…你安慰人的办法也太不同寻常了吧！
t_en: I knew you were joking... You certainly have an unorthodox way of cheering people up!

t_ch: 不过…你说完这句话后，*我们*居然感觉…有些畅快。谢谢你，阿雅。
t_en: However, after you said that, we somehow feel a bit better. Thank you, Agy.

t_ch: 因为我们人性尚存，才会想苦中作乐。
t_en: We find humor in hardship because our humanity still endures.

t_ch: 那…小白他们提到的「渗透」，你有头绪了吗？
t_en: So, about the "infiltration" Snowy mentioned earlier, did you find anything?

t_ch: 放心，即便没有他们的情报，我也已探查到城底的暗流。不过是些小小的麻烦，我能亲自解决。
t_en: Don't worry. Even without their intel, I've already detected hidden currents within the city. A trivial issue, nothing I can't handle on my own.

t_ch: 不用通知圣城守卫吗？
t_en: Do you need to inform the city guards?

t_ch: 那会惊动奥赫玛的公民。假使他们知晓敌人有绕过黄金裔、渗透奥赫玛的手段，局面可能失控。
t_en: That would alarm Okhema's citizens. If they learn that the enemy can bypass the Chrysos Heirs and infiltrate the city, the situation might spiral out of control.

t_ch: 那，你需要*我们*帮什么忙吗？
t_en: Is there anything you'd like us to help with?

t_ch: 不必了，吾师。依照此前的分工来吧，你要为「天谴之锋」做好准备。
t_en: It's fine, Tribbie. As we discussed earlier, you need to prepare for the Blade of Fury.

t_ch: *我们*明白啦…阿雅，你要量力而为呀。
t_en: We got this! Don't exhaust yourself, Agy.

t_ch: 阿雅，你要量力而为呀。
t_en: Don't exhaust yourself, Agy.

t_ch: （渗透…这绝非{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}清醒时会做出的行为。）
t_en: (Infiltration... {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} would never resort to this if they were in their right mind.)

t_ch: （究竟是什么让它丢弃了原本的神性？答案藏在迷雾后…希望遐蝶和那位异邦客能找到根源。）
t_en: (What exactly led to their loss of divinity? The answer lies behind the fog... I hope Castorice and that outlander can uncover the truth.)

t_ch: 衣匠，将若虫收集到的情报交给我吧。
t_en: Garmentmaker, can you give me the intel the nymphs have gathered?

t_ch: （下城…渗透还未殃及浴场，很幸运。风险尚在可控范围之内。）
t_en: (The lower city... It's good that the infiltration hasn't reached the baths yet. The risk remains manageable.)

t_ch: （在悲剧酿成前，要用金线缝补裂口。）
t_en: (The rift must be sewn shut with golden thread before disaster occurs.)

t_ch: 遇见麻烦了吗？
t_en: Something wrong?

t_ch: …是谁？！
t_en: ...Who goes there!?

t_ch: 阿、阿格莱雅大人！您、您怎么会……
t_en: L—Lady Aglaea! Wh—What brings you...

t_ch: 这些「异常」背后藏着腐败和疯狂。我必须亲手清理它们，以绝后患。
t_en: Corruption and madness are lurking behind these anomalies. I must deal with them myself to prevent further troubles.

t_ch: 但我不想因此招致公民的恐慌——我能信任你吗，光荣的卫士？
t_en: But I don't want to alarm the public. Can I trust you, honorable guard?

t_ch: 我明白了，大人！请您放心，我绝不会透露任何风声。
t_en: Absolutely, Lady Aglaea! You have my solemn vow that none of this will come to light.

t_ch: 谢谢你。不介意的话，就把这里交给我吧。
t_en: Thank you. If you don't mind, I will handle it from here.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}祝福您，大人。
t_en: May {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} bless you, Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: （那个年轻的卫士能读懂我的言中之意…若非末日临近，他必然前途无量。）
t_en: (That young guard reads between the lines well. If the end weren't so near, he would have a bright future ahead of him.)

t_ch: （聚焦于眼下的麻烦吧……）
t_en: (I'd better focus on the matter at hand first...)

t_ch: 污秽之物，显形吧——
t_en: Filth, show yourself —

t_ch: 千百根坚韧的金线探入污染的空洞，揪出了潜藏其中的恶意之物。
t_en: Hundreds of resilient golden threads reach into the corrupted void, extracting the hidden malevolence within.

t_ch: 被剥离了荣耀的兵卒，只能如蛆虫般寄生于秽物中——可悲。
t_en: It's truly pathetic. A soldier stripped of all glory, lurking in the margins like a parasite.

t_ch: 回归泥土吧，眷属。
t_en: Return to the dust, Titankin!

t_ch: 你好，孩子。
t_en: Good day, child.

t_ch: …孩子？
t_en: ...Child?

t_ch: 身边的女孩依旧无动于衷。
t_en: The girl beside you remains unresponsive.

t_ch: （啊…原来是个听不见声音的孩子。）
t_en: (Oh... She's a child who can't hear.)

t_ch: （轻轻触碰女孩的肩膀。）
t_en: (Gently touch the girl's shoulder.)

t_ch: （用指尖扫过女孩的长发。）
t_en: (Run your fingertips through the girl's hair.)

t_ch: （伸出手臂。）
t_en: (Hold out your arms.)

t_ch: 阿格莱雅伸出手臂，示意女孩也做出相同的动作。
t_en: Aglaea extends her arm, signaling the girl to do the same.

t_ch: 金色的丝线轻轻地裹住女孩的手掌…现在她们心灵相通了。
t_en: The golden thread gently wraps around the girl's palm... now their hearts are connected.

t_ch: （都告诉我吧，孩子。）
t_en: (Tell me everything, child.)

t_ch: （我的…我的髀石护身符，掉进可怕的东西里面了……）
t_en: (M—My knucklebone talisman fell into this really frightening place...)

t_ch: （很黑，很吓人…我不敢伸手去拿……）
t_en: (It's so dark and scary... I'm too afraid to reach in...)

t_ch: （啊…我明白了。别着急，我来帮你。）
t_en: (Ah... I see. Don't worry, I'll help you find it.)

t_ch: （你只需要帮我一个小忙…转过身去，在心里默念到三十，好吗？）
t_en: (Just do me a little favor, okay? I want you to turn around and count to thirty.)

t_ch: 你收回了金线，该处理眼前的污秽了。
t_en: You retract the threads, ready to deal with the filth before you.

t_ch: 污秽之物，显形吧——
t_en: Show yourself, worm—

t_ch: 千百根坚韧的金线探入污染的空洞，揪出了潜藏其中的恶意之物。
t_en: Hundreds of resilient golden threads reach into the corrupted void, extracting the hidden malevolence within.

t_ch: 连孩童的玩物也不放过…贪婪也是你原罪的一部分吗？
t_en: Even a child's toy isn't safe from your grasp... Is greed one of your vices?

t_ch: 回归泥土吧，秽物。
t_en: Return to the dust, wretch.

t_ch: （你丢的是这个吗？）
t_en: (Is this what you're looking for?)

t_ch: （对，就是它！谢谢你，姐姐……）
t_en: (Yes! That's it! Thank you, big sis!)
(Big sis...)

t_ch: （…你还好吗，姐姐？）
t_en: (...Something on your mind, big sis?)

t_ch: （不…没什么。你要记得，以后不可以独自在街上玩耍…即便是在奥赫玛。）
t_en: (Nothing. But promise me you won't play on the streets alone from now on, even in Okhema.)

t_ch: （你的…「弱点」，会成为他人恶意的靶标。对芸芸广众而言，作恶总比行善代价更小…这是经验之谈。）
t_en: (Your vulnerabilities will make you a target for others' malice. For many, it's easier to do wrong than to do good — believe me, I know from experience.)

t_ch: （姐姐，你说的话好深奥…我听不太懂。）
t_en: (Big sis, your words are so hard to understand. I'm really confused.)

t_ch: （…抱歉，看来我已经忘记了该如何与孩童沟通。你们身上的童真，离我有些远了。）
t_en: (...Sorry, it seems I've forgotten how to talk to a child. Your innocence feels so distant to me now.)

t_ch: （没关系，虽然我听不太懂，但还是谢谢姐姐的提醒！我以后会注意的。）
t_en: (That's alright. I might not understand everything, but I still appreciate your advice. I'll be careful!)

t_ch: （还有，姐姐你…真的好漂亮！简直就像石版上记录的{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}女神一样，金闪闪的！你的眼睛…也像两颗宝石一样，真让人羡慕！）
t_en: (Oh, and by the way, you look so pretty! Just like Goddess {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#} on the teleslate — so shiny and sparkly! Your eyes are like two beautiful gems! I'm so jealous!)

t_ch: （这块髀石护身符，就送给姐姐吧。希望你每天都能有好运气，将来也不要被任何人欺负！）
t_en: (I want you to have this knucklebone talisman, big sis. I hope it brings you luck every day and keeps you safe from bullies!)

t_ch: （…谢谢你，孩子。）
t_en: (...Thank you, child.)

t_ch: （居然在如此隐蔽的角落……）
t_en: (It's hidden in such an unassuming corner...)

t_ch: （假若敌人从这里渗透，可能造成的损害无法估量。尽快处理吧……）
t_en: (If the enemy were to infiltrate from here, the damage could be catastrophic. I'd better deal with it at once...)

t_ch: 千百根坚韧的金线探入污染的空洞，揪出了潜藏其中的……
t_en: Hundreds of resilient golden threads reach into the corrupted void, extracting from within...

t_ch: 哎、哎哟！谁啊，哪个不长眼睛的——
t_en: O—Ouch! Watch where you're going, will you—

t_ch: …巴特鲁斯？
t_en: ...Bartholos?

t_ch: 啊…啊！桀桀桀，原来是阿格莱雅大人！赞美「法刻勒」，您的每一寸肌肤都同那天上黎明机器一样光芒万丈啊！
t_en: O—Oh, it's Lady Aglaea! Teehee, what a delight to see you here! Praise Phakele, every inch of your skin is glowing like the dawn itself!

t_ch: 你还记不住天父之名吗？是刻法——
t_en: How can you still mix up the sky father's name? It's Kepha—

t_ch: …罢了，我想你是故意为之。告诉我，贼灵，你为何要藏身于污秽中？
t_en: ...Never mind. I bet you did that on purpose. Tell me, Spirithief, why are you hiding in the filth?

t_ch: 呃，当然是…当然是因为我们想为奥赫玛的公民除害啦，桀桀桀！
t_en: Uh, it's... it's we're just trying to get rid of any threats to Okhema, haha!

t_ch: 您看，原本藏在里面的疯子石头怪，咱可是已经靠着灵巧的手艺给它大卸八块了…桀桀桀！
t_en: See? We turned this crazy stone monster inside into pieces with our spectacular skills, teehee!

t_ch: 嗯…污染的痕迹确实消失了。
t_en: Hmm... the traces of corruption have indeed disappeared.

t_ch: 我们可不敢骗您啊，阿格莱雅大人~您可是整个奥赫玛最受尊敬的……
t_en: We would never dream of deceiving you, Lady Aglaea. In all of Okhema, you are the most revered—

t_ch: 省下那些油嘴滑舌吧，贼灵…你知道我能读出你的不安。
t_en: Save the smooth talk, Spirithief... I can tell you're feeling nervous.

t_ch: 转念一想，既然你已经在我面前现身…那就再回答我一个问题：<color=#dbc291ff>赛飞儿</color>身在何处？
t_en: On second thought, since you're right here, I must ask you one more question: Where is <color=#dbc291ff>Cipher</color>?

t_ch: 呃…您可能对我们这行有点误解？我和那位大人的确是同行，但这也不代表咱们是一伙的呀！
t_en: Uh... There seems to be a misunderstanding, Lady Aglaea. Even though she and I work in the same trade, it doesn't make us allies!

t_ch: 我俩之间的关系，应该算作「竞争对手」才对——冤家之间可不想狭路相逢呀，桀桀桀！
t_en: On the contrary, we're more like competitors! No competitor would want to cross paths with one another, teehee!

t_ch: …罢了。若你下次遇见赛飞儿，替我转告她：<color=#dbc291ff>身为半神，我们永远不可能逃避职责。</color>
t_en: ...Forget it. If you see her, let her know this: <color=#dbc291ff>As demigods, we can't avoid our duties forever.</color>

t_ch: 好的，好的，我们记下了，阿格莱雅大人！那、那么，要是没什么别的事，我就先走一……
t_en: Alright, alright. Understood, Lady Aglaea! If there's nothing else, please excuse—

t_ch: 等等…依我的推测，你藏身于污秽中莫不是为了藏匿赃物？
t_en: Wait... if my suspicion is correct, are you skulking about in the filth to keep something hidden?

t_ch: 留下你的非法所得，然后我会放你离开。
t_en: Return what you've stolen, and I'll let you go.

t_ch: …嘁，这趟可真倒霉！怎么偏偏就遇上了这么麻烦的人物……
t_en: ...Tsk! What rotten luck! Why did I have to bump into her...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}老祖，请让我抱紧您老的飞毛腿……
t_en: O venerable {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}, may I borrow a fragment of your fleetness?

t_ch: 我奉劝你……
t_en: I urge you—

t_ch: <size=28>…开溜！！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>...Run!!</size>

t_ch: 还有什么解数？尽管使出来吧。
t_en: Got any more tricks up your sleeve? Let's see them all.

t_ch: 可恶…可恶啊！明明就差一点点！
t_en: Argh, darn it! That was so close!

t_ch: 拿走吧！把我辛辛苦苦得来的收获全都拿走吧！
t_en: Go on, take it all! Help yourself to everything I've toiled for!

t_ch: 这次算是栽在你手上了，女人！反正我就算是饿死了，这城里也不会有人愿意给我一个怜悯的眼神！
t_en: Well, you got me! Even if I starve to death, nobody in this city would so much as blink an eye at my plight!

t_ch: 真是可怜…但你压根就不需要进食，不是吗？
t_en: Oh, you poor thing... But you don't actually need to eat at all, do you?

t_ch: 你…你这给一颗黑心镀了金的女人！我不想再看见你了，再见！
t_en: Y—You heartless woman! I never want to see you again, not for all the gold in the world!

t_ch: 再见，巴特鲁斯——别忘了替我给赛飞儿递话。
t_en: Farewell, Bartholos. Be sure to get my message to Cipher.

t_ch: 谁在那？赶在金线还未沾染你的血滴之前，现身吧。
t_en: Show yourself now, before golden thread is stained with your blood.

t_ch: 啧啧，不愧是半神。连我这干了大半辈子「清洗」的刺客也逃不过你的感官啊。
t_en: Well, well, a demigod never ceases to impress. Not even a top assassin with a lifetime of "purging" experiences can slip past you.

t_ch: 凯妮斯阁下，你这是何意？
t_en: What are you up to, Elder Caenis?

t_ch: 呵…不必假装，你早就发现我的眼线了吧？
t_en: Drop the act. You noticed my informants a long time ago, didn't you?

t_ch: 你刻意没有戳穿，为的就是让我们放松警惕，认定你的一举一动皆在元老院的规矩之内——对吗？
t_en: You stayed silent to throw us off, so we'd let our guard down, while maintaining that everything you did abides by the Council's regulations — correct?

t_ch: …要论阴谋心计，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}与元老院相比亦捉襟见肘。
t_en: ...When it comes to schemes and manipulation, {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} can't hold a candle to the Council.

t_ch: 呵呵…承蒙夸赞，黄金裔。
t_en: Hehe, that's quite flattering, Chrysos Heir.

t_ch: 我们没有别的意思，只是想再提醒你一次——<color=#dbc291ff>黄金裔对圣城的管理是临时的，这只是非常时期的权力托管。</color>
t_en: We bear no ill will. We just want to reiterate that <color=#dbc291ff>the Chrysos Heirs' governance of the holy city is only temporary, a provisional transfer of authority during these extraordinary times.</color>

t_ch: 神有神的耳目，人也有人的应对。待到所谓的「神谕」破灭之时…奥赫玛的命运会重新被公民握在手中。
t_en: While gods see and hear more extensively, mortals have their own means of dealing with things. When that so-called prophecy crumbles... the destiny of Okhema shall once again rest in the grasp of its people.

t_ch: 回到公民手中…还是回到你的手中，凯妮斯阁下？
t_en: Rest in the grasp of its people... or yours, Elder Caenis?

t_ch: 不必如此咄咄逼人。当年的决议投票，可是有接近半数的元老拒绝将圣城交到你们手中，我不过是反对派里不起眼的一员。
t_en: Ease off on the hostility. You'd do well to remember nearly half of the elders stood against entrusting the holy city to you in that crucial vote. I am but one voice in the dissent.

t_ch: 接着走你自己的路吧，半神，用尽你的浑身解数来证明我们是错的…但一刻也别忘记，在翁法罗斯，即便是「神」也不能为所欲为。
t_en: Keep following your chosen path, demigod, use every bit of your power to show that we are mistaken... but don't forget for a second, in Amphoreus, even "gods" are not above the law.

t_ch: 我也想看看，你这事事皆要亲力亲为的行事风格能坚持多久…呵呵。
t_en: I'm quite interested to see how long you can stick with this hands-on way of doing things... heh.

t_ch: 我会尽己所能拯救翁法罗斯，凯妮斯阁下。不论是从诸神手中……
t_en: I will employ every means at my disposal to save Amphoreus, Elder Caenis. Whether it means wresting it from the grasp of the gods...

t_ch: 还是从你们手中。
t_en: Or yours.

t_ch: 城中的威胁已经排除了，我已将污秽逐一清理。
t_en: The dangers lurking within Okhema have been eradicated. I've also cleansed the city of filth.

t_ch: 感谢你，阿雅。那位元老…她又给你添麻烦了吗？
t_en: Thank you, Agy. That elder just now... Was she causing you trouble again?

t_ch: 并无大碍。不必为我担心，吾师。
t_en: Worry not. I'm capable of handling it, Trinnon.

t_ch: 你应该知道的，凯妮斯，她在黄金战争时……
t_en: You should know that during the Chrysos War, Caenis was...

t_ch: …她是专职「清洗」黄金裔的刺客。我明白。
t_en: ...An assassin known for purging the Chrysos Heirs. I am aware.

t_ch: 如我所言，元老院并非我们的敌人…至少现在不是。还是回归迫在眉睫的议题吧。
t_en: Like I mentioned before, the Council is not our adversary... at least not now. We should be focusing on the more pressing matter at hand.

t_ch: …嗯。我们已经做好了应对{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的准备。这并不容易…缇安已经接近极限了。
t_en: ...You're right. We've taken all possible precautions against {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}. It... did not come easy, though... Trianne is already near her limits.

t_ch: 她还剩下多少力量？
t_en: ...How much of her power is left?

t_ch: 算上这一次的话…<color=#dbc291ff>也许还能开启三…不，还能开启两次「百界门」吧。</color>
t_en: Taking this time into account, <color=#dbc291ff>she should still be able to open the Century Gates three... no, maybe just two more times.</color>

t_ch: …那之后呢？
t_en: ...And after that?

t_ch: 依照先前的约定，缇宝会接过「门匠」的职责。
t_en: Tribbie will take over as the gatekeeper, according to what we've agreed on.

t_ch: 看来，我们现在能做的就只有等待了。
t_en: Waiting seems to be our only plan now.

t_ch: 嗯。就在这花园静候片刻吧，阿雅。
t_en: Indeed. Let us wait here in this garden, Agy.

t_ch: 请告诉我，吾师……
t_en: Please enlighten me, Trinnon...

t_ch: 在你眼中的世界，预言仍如第一日那般清晰吗？
t_en: In the world as you perceive it, does the prophecy remain as clear as it was when it first descended?

t_ch: 金色的符文在碧空中闪烁，数不清的只言片语在{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}身边起舞。神谕一如既往地清晰。
t_en: Golden runes shimmer against the azure sky, and myriad fragments of words dance around {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}. The prophecy remains as clear as ever.

t_ch: 它诉说了白厄一行的胜利吗？
t_en: Does it foretell the triumph of Phainon and the others?

t_ch: 它…缄口不言。它仿佛知道答案…却不想在这一时刻同我们分享。
t_en: It... remains silent, seemingly cognizant of the outcome... though it's unwilling to let us in on the answer just yet.

t_ch: 看来这一次，我们无法从神谕口中得到慰藉了。
t_en: We're not going to be finding our solace in the prophecy this time, then.

t_ch: 如此也好。若他果真是被命运选中的孩子……
t_en: So be it. If indeed he is fate's chosen child...

t_ch: …那预言也不过是他故事的旁白。
t_en: ...then the prophecy is nothing more than a footnote to his tale.

t_ch: 悬锋城……
t_en: Castrum Kremnos...

t_ch: 在{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}闭目之前，它原来是这副模样。
t_en: So this is how it looked like before {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} closed their eyes.

t_ch: 该从哪里找起呢？
t_en: Where should we start searching?

t_ch: 真是壮观。
t_en: What a magnificent sight.

t_ch: 战士之城，它的壮观名副其实。
t_en: The City of Warriors, its magnificence certainly lives up to its name.

t_ch: 首先，得弄清我们回到了哪个时代。
t_en: We should first figure out which era we've traveled back to.

t_ch: 为迷迷收集的记忆全都指向黄金战争的尾声，或许能以此为基点来做推断。
t_en: The memory fragments we gathered for Mem all point toward the final stage of the Chrysos War... We might be able to use that as a starting point for our deductions.

t_ch: 直接问问不就行了！
t_en: But we can just ask around!

t_ch: 怕是…会被当做可疑的人吧？悬锋城不是闲游之地，我们不该表现得太过显眼。
t_en: ...We may draw suspicion this way. Castrum Kremnos is no leisure spot for tourists. We shouldn't draw unnecessary attention to ourselves.

t_ch: 这就是当穿越者的感觉吗？
t_en: Ooh, so this is what it feels like to be a time traveler?

t_ch: 的确…有些奇妙。悬锋城不是闲游之地，我们不该表现得太过显眼。
t_en: It's... quite intriguing, indeed. But Castrum Kremnos is no leisure spot for tourists. We shouldn't draw unnecessary attention to ourselves.

t_ch: 先在外围调查一番吧，或许能从只言片语中获取有用的信息。
t_en: Let's start with the city's surroundings. We might be able to gather some clues by listening and observing.

t_ch: …您好？
t_en: ...Hello?

t_ch: 在吗？
t_en: Anyone home?
Heellooo!

t_ch: 听不见吗？
t_en: Is he hard of hearing?

t_ch: …何事？
t_en: ...What is it?

t_ch: 请问，您是来参加竞赛的吗？
t_en: ...May I ask if you're here to join the competition?

t_ch: 竞赛…？
t_en: Competition...?

t_ch: 不…我对无聊的运动并无兴趣。
t_en: No... I have no interest in meaningless sports.

t_ch: 那…您是为了{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的祭典而来？
t_en: So... you're here for {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s festival?

t_ch: 愚蠢至极……
t_en: Utterly foolish...

t_ch: 真是个怪人…
t_en: What a cranky guy...

t_ch: 在那个年代，因战争留下创伤的人并不少见。不用太过介意……
t_en: War has left its mark on many from this period, so please don't take it personally...

t_ch: 「王储」？「黄金裔」？笑话！
t_en: Crown prince? Chrysos Heir? What a joke!

t_ch: 迈德漠斯是个懦夫！随他离开悬锋城的人愧对{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}的名…耻辱的逃亡者，此生将无法再沐浴荣光！
t_en: Mydeimos is just a coward! Those who deserted Kremnos with him have brought shame upon the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#}... Despicable traitors will never bask in glory again!

t_ch: 但迈德漠斯确实是我们之中最强大的战士…如今他也选择了离开，我们还能追随谁？
t_en: But Mydeimos is undoubtedly the mightiest warrior among us... Who else can we turn to now that he's gone?

t_ch: 你在胡言乱语什么？王欧利庞——悬锋城真正的领导者——还没有倒下！他仍坚守在这座城邦，也未曾放弃唤醒伟大的泰坦。
t_en: What nonsense are you spouting? King Eurypon, the rightful ruler of Castrum Kremnos, has not fallen! He still stands firm in this city and has never given up on awakening the great Titan.

t_ch: 只要坚定地站在他身边，我们就能找回昨日的荣光，继续给敌人带去血光和征服……
t_en: As long as we remain steadfast at his side, we'll reclaim our former glory and sow bloodshed and conquest amongst our foes...

t_ch: 王欧利庞……
t_en: King Eurypon...?

t_ch: 啊，当然…当时的人们还不知道那位旧王的结局。
t_en: Right, of course... The people of this time wouldn't know the fate of that old king yet.

t_ch: 万敌是个…逃兵？
t_en: Mydeimos... is a deserter?

t_ch: 不…我不认为万敌阁下的出走是出于怯懦…相反，那是勇敢的决定。
t_en: No... I don't believe that Lord Mydei left out of cowardice. It was, in fact, a courageous choice.

t_ch: 这些人好像很愤怒。
t_en: Those people sound furious.

t_ch: 他们有理由愤慨…因为万敌阁下曾是悬锋城的英雄。
t_en: They are entitled to that rage... because Lord Mydei was once the hero of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 但他并非因为怯懦出走…相反，那是勇敢的决定。
t_en: But he did not leave out of cowardice... It was, in fact, a courageous choice.

t_ch: 什么结局？
t_en: What fate?

t_ch: …被万敌阁下杀死的结局。
t_en: ...He was killed by Lord Mydei.

t_ch: 如此一来，我们所处的时代已经比较明朗了。
t_en: With that, we should have enough to determine the time period we are currently in.

t_ch: 是用通用语书写的卷轴，读读看吧。
t_en: It's a scroll written in the common tongue. We should see what it's about.

t_ch: 「勇气，荣耀，理智，坚韧，牺牲」……
t_en: Courage, Honor, Reason, Tenacity, Sacrifice...

t_ch: 鼎盛时的{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}配得上这些词语。但现在……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} did live up to these values in their prime, but now...

t_ch: 看来此时的悬锋城正在举办祭典，胜出的战士可以觐见城主，获得荣耀。和达米亚诺斯先生说的一样。
t_en: Kremnos seems to be hosting a festival, where its victor can go on to have an audience with the ruler and be bestowed with honor. It's just as Mr. Damionis described.

t_ch: 如果能假扮成参赛者，我们或许可以进入城内……
t_en: If we can pose as participants, we might be able to get into the city...

t_ch: 上等武器！都来看啊，悬锋城最锋利的武器！
t_en: Finest weapons for sale! Come take a look! The sharpest blades in all of Kremnos!

t_ch: 大工匠哈托努斯亲手打造的角斗剑！锤矛！圆盾！
t_en: Gladiator swords, maces, and bucklers crafted by the Grand Craftsman Chartonus himself!

t_ch: 想在悬锋祭典上杀敌如麻？来我这里购入最上等的武器吧！
t_en: Looking to breeze through your foes at the Kremnos Festival? I've got the best weapons right here for you!

t_ch: 生意好像不怎么样啊？
t_en: Not getting much business, huh?

t_ch: 呵…那帮斗士都是群粗人，压根就不识货！
t_en: Hmph... Those boorish warriors know nothing about quality gear!

t_ch: 真是哈托努斯打造的？
t_en: Are these really crafted by Chartonus?

t_ch: 童叟无欺！要怪就怪那帮粗野的斗士，压根就不识货！
t_en: I'm as honest a merchant as they come! Those boorish warriors know nothing about quality gear!

t_ch: 但您就不一样了！瞅您这气质，肯定是来自哪个大城邦的名家贵族吧？来来来，我的存货，给您过过眼……
t_en: Ah, but you're not like them! With your air of sophistication, you must be a noble from one of the grand city-states, right? Here, let me show you what I have in stock...

t_ch: （望向遐蝶）
t_en: (Look at Castorice.)

t_ch: 不会被骗吧？
t_en: Could this be a scam?

t_ch: 从武器的制式可以判别年代…浅浅浏览一下商品也无妨。
t_en: The craftsmanship of a weapon reveals the time period it's made... We can afford to take a quick look.

t_ch: 虽然我们身处记忆中，{NICKNAME}阁下…但还是克制下消费的欲望吧？
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}... Much as we're in a memory of the past, impulse buying is still an undesirable behavior.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，您出手可真是大方！欢迎惠顾，下次再来！
t_en: Wahaha, you're quite the high-roller! Thank you, and please come again!

t_ch: 可以让我看看那把武器吗，{NICKNAME}阁下？
t_en: May I take a look at that weapon, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我本以为你是会冲动消费的类型，{NICKNAME}阁下…有些刮目相看了。
t_en: I thought you'd be the kind to make impulsive purchases, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss.} {NICKNAME}, but you've certainly proven me wrong.

t_ch: 呃…只看不买？真浪费时间……
t_en: Tsk. So you're just window shopping? What a waste of my time...

t_ch: 商人阁下，介意让我看看你手上的武器吗？
t_en: Mister, can I please have a look at the weapon you're holding on to?

t_ch: 哦？这位恬静的女士居然对兵器感兴趣？可以可以，请随便看！
t_en: Oh? A gentle lady like you taking an interest in weapons? Why, of course! Please, feel free to take a closer look!

t_ch: 遐蝶小心翼翼地接过武器，放在手中仔细端详了片刻……
t_en: Castorice carefully takes the weapon in her hand and examines it closely for a moment...

t_ch: …我看过了。走吧，{NICKNAME}阁下。
t_en: ...I've seen enough. Let's go, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss.} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 嗯？不再看看别的货了吗？
t_en: Leaving already? Don't you want to browse through my other wares?

t_ch: 那…两位，要是你们能活过今天的祭典，欢迎下次再来光临。
t_en: Well then, come back anytime... if you make it through the festival today.

t_ch: 你看出什么了吗？
t_en: What's wrong with that weapon?

t_ch: 刚才那是什么意思？
t_en: What was that just now?

t_ch: 那些武器上有血腥味。他很努力地掩盖，但死亡的痕迹没那么容易清除…至于那些商品是哪来的，我不愿联想。
t_en: Those weapons reek of blood. He made an effort to conceal it, but traces of death aren't something you can easily erase... I shudder to even consider where he got his goods from.

t_ch: 但他没有撒谎，那把兵器的确出自哈托努斯之手。从上面的铭刻，我大致能推断出所处的年代。
t_en: He didn't lie about the weapon being one of Chartonus' handiwork, though. Thanks to its inscription, I now have a fairly good guess of the time period we're in.

t_ch: 上等武器！都来看啊，悬锋城最锋利的武器！
t_en: Finest weapons for sale! Come take a look! The sharpest blades in all of Kremnos!

t_ch: 等等，{NICKNAME}阁下，最好先别去那边……
t_en: Please wait, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}. It's best not to go there yet...

t_ch: 还是先在附近收集下线索吧。
t_en: Let's try to find more clues around here first.

t_ch: 总结下现有的信息，{NICKNAME}阁下。
t_en: Let's run through everything we know so far, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 万敌阁下已经带着族人投奔了奥赫玛；旧王欧利庞仍然在世，但{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}却陷入了沉眠；悬锋城正在召集勇士，召开祭典……
t_en: Lord Mydei has gone to seek refuge in Okhema with some of his people. Old King Eurypon still reigns, though {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} has fallen into a slumber. And Castrum Kremnos is now gathering warriors from all over in preparation for their festival...

t_ch: 看来我们回到了黄金战争的末年，悬锋城正面临内部分化和泰坦失神的两重打击。
t_en: It seems that we've traveled back to the last years of the Chrysos War, a time when Castrum Kremnos grappled with both internal strife and the Titan's waning divinity.

t_ch: 你有什么计划吗？
t_en: Do you have a plan in mind?

t_ch: 不废话，直接杀进去！
t_en: Talk is cheap. Let's charge in!

t_ch: 这么做风险太大，阁下…何况，我可能会变成你的累赘。
t_en: That's too risky of a plan. And I might even become a burden to you.

t_ch: 不使用下你的能力吗？
t_en: It's about time you used your powers.

t_ch: 我…无法那么精确地控制力量，这么做风险太大。
t_en: I... can't control my strength with that much precision, the risk is too great.

t_ch: 最稳妥的方式还是融入规则…既然城中正在举办祭典，也许我们可以前去报名。
t_en: The safest bet is to play by the rules. They happen to be hosting a festival in the city... We can try entering as participants?

t_ch: 先进入城中，再找机会行动吧。必要时…我会解决{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的眷属。
t_en: Let's make our way into the city first, we can then wait for the right opportunity to strike. When it comes down to it... I will take care of {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s kin.

t_ch: 那里应该就是报名点。
t_en: That should be the place to sign up.

t_ch: …多么可惜！那三位斗士只差一点便能通过试炼，觐见雄伟的欧利庞王，摘得悬锋勇士的桂冠……
t_en: ...Such a pity! Those three warriors were so close to completing the trial, to emerging as victorious Kremnoan warriors, and to earning an audience with our glorious King Eurypon!

t_ch: 但他们还是倒在了最后一步！天谴先锋手起刀落，将他们剁成了肉泥…愿{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}承认他们的骁勇，接收他们的英灵。
t_en: And yet, they collapsed at the final hurdle! The Furiae Praetor swiftly hacked them down, reducing them to mere mincemeat... May {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} recognize their valor and embrace their heroic spirits.

t_ch: …下一组！下一组前来挑战的斗士在哪？欧利庞王还在等待，等待第一位能活着站在他面前的勇士！
t_en: ...Next! Do we have any more warriors ready for the challenge? King Eurypon awaits, longing for the first brave soul to survive and stand before him!

t_ch: 这竞赛听着挺费人啊…
t_en: This competition sounds pretty brutal...

t_ch: 这项目这么硬核吗？
t_en: Is this competition really that hardcore?

t_ch: 那是生命比面包更廉价的年代，人们见惯了蛮荒和死亡。
t_en: This was a time when life was valued far less than bread. People hardly bat their eyelids at the savagery and death that had become commonplace.

t_ch: 没人胆敢迎战吗？你们难道不愿赌上荣耀，在悬锋的光辉下燃烧自己的生命吗？
t_en: Does no one have the courage to take up the challenge? Are you not prepared to wager your glory and set your lives ablaze under the splendor of Kremnos?

t_ch: …让我过去。
t_en: ...Let me through.

t_ch: 又是你，话都说不清的愚氓！你手无寸铁，也妄想加入悬锋祭典？这是对{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}的不敬！
t_en: Why, it's you again, inarticulate fool! Pfft, unarmed, and yet you dare to think you can join the Kremnos Festival? That's an insult to the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 我已强调过了——这是同时考验三人的试炼！若是连愿意和你出生入死的同伴都找不到，还是尽早回家放牧吧！
t_en: I've told you before — this is a trial for three! This is a trial that requires three people! If you can't even gather enough comrades to stand by you in life and death, it's time you went home and took up herding!

t_ch: …让我过去。
t_en: ...Let me through.

t_ch: 看来你不止是口齿不清，连耳朵也不怎么好使…你如果继续胡搅蛮缠，休怪我们不客气。
t_en: Hmph, so you're not just inarticulate, but also hard of hearing... Keep up this act of nuisance, and I'll be sure to show you how uncivil we can get!

t_ch: 三人组队……
t_en: Teams of three...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，这也许是个机会…我们可以和那个人一起进城。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, this might be our chance...! We can enter the city with him.

t_ch: 我要报名！
t_en: Sign me up!

t_ch: 我们两个是他的队友！
t_en: We'll be his teammates!

t_ch: 嗯？你们是……
t_en: Hm? Who are...

t_ch: …你们。
t_en: ...You two.

t_ch: 呵…呵呵，我大概明白了。
t_en: Pfft... I see what this is.

t_ch: 无妨！既然你找到了愿意一同赴死的战友，那便上前来吧，愚氓！
t_en: As you wish! Good for you, you now have allies to court death with. Come on up here, fool!

t_ch: 血脉偾张的戏码已经快让人审美疲劳了，加入一组实力相差悬殊的「插曲」亦是不错的调剂。
t_en: The adrenaline-pumping scenes are becoming a bit tiresome, so adding a wildly mismatched "interlude" would make for a refreshing change and spice things up.

t_ch: 来吧——「生存」就是唯一的规则！只要你们还有一丝呼吸，要么克服所有难关，赢得此世的荣耀——要么把灵魂留在悬锋城，成为「纷争」的养料！
t_en: Let the trial begin, then! Your only goal is to survive! As long as you still hold the breath of life, you'll either triumph over every trial and claim worldly glory — or leave your soul in Kremnos to become fuel for Strife!

t_ch: 赌注有点大…
t_en: The stakes are high...

t_ch: 我不确定…
t_en: I'm not sure about this...

t_ch: 要坚定，{NICKNAME}阁下…想想白厄和万敌，我们也要拿出同样的决心。
t_en: Have faith, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}... Lord Phainon and Lord Mydei are still fighting. We must match their unwavering resolve.

t_ch: …可以进去了吗？
t_en: ...Can we enter now?

t_ch: 这么着急赴死吗？很好，那就让试炼开始吧！
t_en: Eager to meet your demise? Very well then, let the trial begin!

t_ch: 报上你们的名字，勇士们。
t_en: Give me your names, warriors.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 遐蝶。
t_en: Castorice.

t_ch: …格奈乌斯。
t_en: ...Gnaeus.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，遐蝶，格奈乌斯……
t_en: Castorice, Gnaeus, and {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 银河球棒侠！
t_en: I am the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 遐蝶。
t_en: Castorice.

t_ch: …格奈乌斯。
t_en: ...Gnaeus.

t_ch: 银河球棒侠，遐蝶，格奈乌斯……
t_en: Galactic Baseballer, Castorice, and Gnaeus...

t_ch: 能借着天谴之锋的耀光看清各位的面庞，我倍感荣幸。
t_en: It is an honor to see your true faces revealed by the radiant glow of The Blade of Fury.

t_ch: 「勇气，荣耀，理智，坚韧，牺牲」——以{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的五大美德之名，穿过圣城的大门，去迎接你们的末路。
t_en: In the name of the five virtues of {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} — courage, honor, reason, tenacity, and sacrifice — stride through the holy city's gate and embrace your fate.

t_ch: 愿你们的战魂能取悦{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}，成为悬锋城征战的养料。
t_en: May your courageous souls satisfy {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} and fuel the conquests of Kremnos.

t_ch: 以{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的五大美德之名，穿过圣城的大门，去迎接你们的末路。
t_en: In the name of the five virtues of {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, stride through the holy city's gate and embrace your fate.

t_ch: 愿你们的战魂能取悦{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}，成为悬锋城征战的养料。
t_en: May your courageous souls satisfy {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} and fuel the conquests of Kremnos.

t_ch: 似乎没有太多守卫。我本以为场面会更加…激烈。
t_en: They have fewer guards than I expected. I thought we'd be greeted with something more... intense.

t_ch: 先礼后兵，还挺讲究。
t_en: Observing formalities before aggression, now that's a thoughtful approach.

t_ch: 战斗民族也喜欢解谜？
t_en: Do these battle-hardened people also have a taste for puzzles?

t_ch: 不明所以……
t_en: Meaningless...

t_ch: 让注定赴死之人浪费时间，用意何在？
t_en: Why stall those charging for Death's door?

t_ch: 我们没有赴死的打算，阁下。想办法解开他们布置的难题吧。
t_en: Death is not part of our plan, Lord Gnaeus. We should work on solving the puzzle they've set for us.

t_ch: 看，那面墙壁上悬挂着两个火盆。也许…这是某种提示？
t_en: Look, there are two braziers on that wall. Could they be... some sort of hint?
